Health_Department_Name: Madera County Department of Public Health

Health_Department_Name: Madera County Department of Public Health
Program_Description: HFA works with pregnant and parenting families of children prenatally up to age 3, aiming to cultivate nurturing parent-child relationships and enhance family functioning.
Target_Population: Low-income families with newborns up to 3 months; pregnant and parenting families.
Eligibility_Requirements: Must be enrolled within the first three months after an infant's birth.
Application_Process: Call to enroll or fill out a form.
Notes: The program does not match any known maternal health programs from the registry.

Health_Department_Name: Madera County Department of Public Health
Program_Description: An evidence-based, voluntary program for pregnant and new parents, aiming to support positive health development and well-being outcomes.
Target_Population: Pregnant or parenting individuals or caregivers of children three months or younger.
Eligibility_Requirements: Must be a member of a CalWORKs assistance unit or think you might be eligible for CalWORKs aid.
Application_Process: Contact Madera County Department of Public Health for more information.
Notes: The program does not match any known maternal health programs from the registry.

Health_Department_Name: Madera County Department of Public Health
Program_Description: Medi-Cal is a state health program that offers no-cost or low-cost health coverage to California.
Target_Population: Pregnant women, families with children, and low-income individuals.
Eligibility_Requirements: Qualified people can enroll in Medi-Cal year-round; higher income allowed for pregnant women.
Application_Process: Visit the Department of Health Care Services website for a step-by-step guide.
Notes: The program does not match any known maternal health programs from the registry.

Health_Department_Name: Madera County Department of Public Health
Program_Description: WIC staff will see if you are eligible by checking various requirements and conducting a health screening.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to 5 years old.
Eligibility_Requirements: Identification for you and any children from birth to 5 years old, proof of current address, proof of income, proof of pregnancy or pregnancy loss.
Application_Process: To make a WIC appointment, please call us at (559) 675-7623.
Required_Documentation: Proof of identity, proof of income, proof of pregnancy or pregnancy loss, proof of current address.
Notes: The program does not match any known maternal health programs from the registry.

Health_Department_Name: Madera County Department of Public Health
Program_Description: WIC is a supplemental nutrition program that helps pregnant women, new mothers, and young children eat well and stay healthy.
Target_Population: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or just had a baby; children under 5 years old including foster children; families with a low to medium income.
Eligibility_Requirements: Families with a low to medium income (185% or less of the federal poverty level). Working families may qualify.
Application_Process: Call (559) 675-7893 to make an appointment and find out which documents you need to bring with you.
Notes: The program does not match any known maternal health programs from the registry.
